config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, it becomes too easy for governments or majorities to abuse power. In my view, society is strongest when basic rights like freedom, dignity, and due process are protected for everyone, even when that is inconvenient. From my perspective, the common good matters, but it should be pursued without sacrificing fundamental individual rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa cepatnya “demi kebaikan bersama” bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti keamanan dan kesehatan, tetapi hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum yang adil, dan perlindungan dari diskriminasi tidak boleh dijadikan korban hanya karena mayoritas menginginkannya. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dijaga, bukan dari individu yang terus dikorbankan atas nama kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “for the common good” can become an excuse for paternalism, pressure from the majority, or even abuse by authorities, so I am cautious about giving society a blanket higher status. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights should be treated as absolute in every case, because public safety, disaster response, and social order sometimes require real limits. The key point is that any restriction on individual rights must be narrow, necessary, and accountable, not justified just because society says so.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus berada di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya di Amerika Serikat, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan itu berbahaya karena mayoritas tidak selalu benar. Saya setuju bahwa ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau keadaan darurat di mana hak individu bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling serius melindungi hak dasar semua orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how quickly “for the common good” can turn into social pressure, paternalism, or even abuse by authorities and majorities, so I do not trust any system that places society above the person by default. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are unlimited; public safety, disaster response, and order can justify narrow restrictions, but only when they are truly necessary and accountable. A strong society is one that protects basic rights first and only limits them carefully, not one that treats individuals as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan berlebihan, dan saya tidak percaya mayoritas otomatis lebih benar daripada individu. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti keamanan publik atau bencana di mana hak bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, perlu, dan bisa diaudit. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan orang, melainkan yang paling konsisten melindungi hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
